Database¶

Connection¶

Database connection and session management.

async pilotlog.database.connection.get_db() → AsyncGenerator[AsyncSession, None][source]¶

Dependency for getting database sessions.

pilotlog.database.connection.get_session() → AsyncGenerator[AsyncSession, None][source]¶

Context manager for database sessions.

async pilotlog.database.connection.init_db() → None[source]¶

Initialize the database, creating tables if they don’t exist.

pilotlog.database.connection.set_sqlite_pragma(dbapi_connection, connection_record)[source]¶

Enable SQLite WAL mode and foreign keys.

Models¶

SQLAlchemy models for PilotLog database.

class pilotlog.database.models.Airport(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Base

Airport lookup data with coordinates.

city: Mapped[str | None]¶
country: Mapped[str | None]¶
iata: Mapped[str | None]¶
icao: Mapped[str]¶
latitude: Mapped[float | None]¶
longitude: Mapped[float | None]¶
name: Mapped[str | None]¶
timezone: Mapped[str | None]¶
class pilotlog.database.models.Base(**kwargs: Any)[source]¶

Bases: DeclarativeBase

Base class for all SQLAlchemy models.

metadata: ClassVar[MetaData] = MetaData()¶

Refers to the _schema.MetaData collection that will be used for new _schema.Table objects.

See also

orm_declarative_metadata

registry: ClassVar[registry] = <sqlalchemy.orm.decl_api.registry object>¶

Refers to the _orm.registry in use where new _orm.Mapper objects will be associated.

class pilotlog.database.models.Flight(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Base

A single flight record.

aircraft_type: Mapped[str | None]¶
aircraft_type_raw: Mapped[str | None]¶
arrival_time: Mapped[int | None]¶
attributes: Mapped[list[FlightAttribute]]¶
block_minutes: Mapped[int]¶
created_at: Mapped[datetime]¶
crew_id: Mapped[str | None]¶
crew_name: Mapped[str | None]¶
crew_position: Mapped[str | None]¶
departure_time: Mapped[int | None]¶
destination: Mapped[str]¶
flight_date: Mapped[str]¶
flight_number: Mapped[str | None]¶
id: Mapped[int]¶
import_batch: Mapped[ImportBatch | None]¶
import_batch_id: Mapped[str | None]¶
is_deadhead: Mapped[bool]¶
origin: Mapped[str]¶
pic_landing: Mapped[bool]¶
pic_takeoff: Mapped[bool]¶
remarks: Mapped[str | None]¶
source: Mapped[str]¶
tail_number: Mapped[str | None]¶
updated_at: Mapped[datetime]¶
class pilotlog.database.models.FlightAttribute(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Base

Flexible key-value attributes for sparse/source-specific flight data.

attribute_name: Mapped[str]¶
attribute_unit: Mapped[str | None]¶
attribute_value: Mapped[str]¶
flight: Mapped[Flight]¶
flight_id: Mapped[int]¶
id: Mapped[int]¶
class pilotlog.database.models.ImportBatch(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Base

Track import operations for audit and rollback.

filename: Mapped[str | None]¶
flights: Mapped[list[Flight]]¶
id: Mapped[str]¶
imported_at: Mapped[datetime]¶
rows_duplicate: Mapped[int | None]¶
rows_imported: Mapped[int | None]¶
rows_processed: Mapped[int | None]¶
rows_skipped: Mapped[int | None]¶
source: Mapped[str]¶
class pilotlog.database.models.SchemaVersion(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Base

Track database schema version for migrations.

applied_at: Mapped[datetime]¶
version: Mapped[int]¶

Queries¶

Database query functions for PilotLog.

async pilotlog.database.queries.check_duplicate_flight(session: AsyncSession, flight_date: str, flight_number: str | None, origin: str, destination: str) → bool[source]¶

Check if a flight already exists (for duplicate detection during import).

async pilotlog.database.queries.get_airport_by_icao(session: AsyncSession, icao: str) → Airport | None[source]¶

Get an airport by ICAO code.

async pilotlog.database.queries.get_airport_statistics(session: AsyncSession, date_from: date | None = None, date_to: date | None = None) → list[dict][source]¶

Get airport visit statistics.

async pilotlog.database.queries.get_airports(session: AsyncSession) → list[Airport][source]¶

Get all airports.

async pilotlog.database.queries.get_career_statistics(session: AsyncSession, date_from: date | None = None, date_to: date | None = None) → dict[source]¶

Get overall career statistics.

async pilotlog.database.queries.get_flight_by_id(session: AsyncSession, flight_id: int) → Flight | None[source]¶

Get a single flight by ID.

async pilotlog.database.queries.get_flights(session: AsyncSession, date_from: date | None = None, date_to: date | None = None, origin: str | None = None, destination: str | None = None, crew: str | None = None, tail: str | None = None, aircraft_type: str | None = None, limit: int = 100, offset: int = 0) → tuple[list[Flight], int][source]¶

Query flights with optional filters.

async pilotlog.database.queries.get_import_batch(session: AsyncSession, batch_id: str) → ImportBatch | None[source]¶

Get an import batch by ID.

async pilotlog.database.queries.get_rolling_totals(session: AsyncSession, as_of_date: date, windows: list[int] = [7, 28, 60, 90, 365]) → dict[int, dict][source]¶

Calculate rolling window totals.

Note: The 28-day (672-hour) window uses >= to include the cutoff date, matching FAR 117 interpretation. Other windows use > for standard calendar-day lookback behavior.

async pilotlog.database.queries.get_route_statistics(session: AsyncSession, date_from: date | None = None, date_to: date | None = None) → list[dict][source]¶

Get aggregated route statistics for map visualization.

async pilotlog.database.queries.get_statistics_by_aircraft_type(session: AsyncSession, date_from: date | None = None, date_to: date | None = None) → list[dict][source]¶

Get statistics grouped by aircraft type.

async pilotlog.database.queries.get_statistics_by_year(session: AsyncSession) → list[dict][source]¶

Get statistics grouped by year.
